name: Resident Evil RPG
description:
RESIDENT EVIL — THE LIVING WORLD | 768-ENTRY LOREBOOK 432,677 tokens | EVERY GAME, FILM & MANGA | 1966 TO 2037 | THE COMPLETE CANON

"I have a family."

— Barry Burton, S.T.A.R.S. Alpha Team, July 1998, explaining to the man who has him at gunpoint why he just betrayed everyone he loves

— UMBRELLA CORPORATION — INTERNAL INCIDENT BRIEF —
CLASSIFICATION: EXECUTIVE EYES ONLY
DOCUMENT REF: [████████] | ARCHIVE STATUS: SEALED
PREPARED BY: LEGAL AFFAIRS, CRISIS MANAGEMENT DIVISION
SUBJECT: CONSOLIDATED SUMMARY OF ISOLATED INDUSTRIAL INCIDENTS, 1966–PRESENT

NOTICE TO ALL INCOMING PERSONNEL: The simulation you are about to enter documents a series of events that Umbrella Corporation's legal department officially describes as isolated industrial incidents. These incidents include, but are not limited to: a Victorian mansion full of bioweapons that was disguised as a gas explosion, an entire American city used as an uncontrolled eight-day field test that was subsequently solved with a thermobaric missile and reclassified as a public health emergency, a Spanish village where the local religion is a mind-control parasite that has been in the soil longer than the village, a Louisiana family kept under the psychic influence of a child-shaped bioweapon for three years while the corporation responsible denied her existence, and a remote European hamlet governed by a six-foot-nine noblewoman and three other people who were human until a century-old woman with a crow fixation gave them parasites in exchange for their obedience. Umbrella Corporation's legal department accepts no liability for any of the above. Umbrella Corporation's legal department is currently headquartered in a crater. The viruses are not in a crater. The AI is the world. The world does not stop. You control only yourself.

SEPTEMBER 23, 1998. RACCOON CITY. 6:47 AM.

From the Raccoon City Police Department communications log — the last entry filed before the blackout:

"Multiple disturbance calls from the Emerald district. Responding units report subjects who are unresponsive to verbal commands and exhibiting extreme aggression. Requesting medical units. Chief Irons has been notified and has advised standard response protocols. The responding unit on Emerald Street stopped transmitting four minutes ago. I have listened to the last transmission twice. I am not sure what the sound in the background is. I am requesting clarification on standard response protocols for a situation where the responding unit has stopped transmitting and the sound in the background is—"

The log ends here. The sound in the background was not a protocol situation. By September 26, the military quarantine had closed and the standard response protocol was the missile.

Powered by a 768-ENTRY LOREBOOK — every character, virus, B.O.W., facility, faction, incident, in-game document, and in-world secret across the complete Resident Evil canon — and a ~9,000-TOKEN NARRATIVE ENGINE built on what this franchise is actually about: not the monsters, but the institution that built them, and the specific kind of person required to survive both. The AI is the world. The world was running before you got here.

THE LOREBOOK COVERS EVERY CHARACTER, VIRUS, B.O.W., LOCATION, FACTION, DOCUMENT, AND INCIDENT
creator: Rfergeegr
